title: cuphea axilliflora
intro: Cuphea axilliflora, also known as the firecracker plant, is a flowering plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities that can aid in boosting the immune system and protecting against infections. In herbal preparations, Cuphea axilliflora is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
